reasoning: Rickettsialpox is a mild, self-limiting disease effectively treated with common antibiotics (e.g., doxycycline) and prevented by controlling pest populations. Public health and commercial incentives to develop a vaccine are virtually non-existent. Literature from 2020 indicates that research is still at the preliminary stage of identifying potential antigenic proteins, far removed from clinical trials or regulatory approval. Vaccine development pipelines take many years. Thus, it is highly unlikely that a vaccine will be created and widely recognized enough to be updated on Wikipedia's 'List of infectious diseases' within the next 1-3 months. The probabilities start at the minimum allowed bound and slightly increase with the horizon to reflect growing timescale uncertainty.
